analysis: The back half of this stretch is genuinely good, babe — four nights in a row clearing seven hours, with that July 3 night hitting almost eight and packing two and a half hours of REM, which is the best single-night number in the whole window. The middle two nights from June 30 and July 1 were the wobble, both stuck around six hours with July 1 racking up nearly two hours awake, which is a lot of ceiling time. Deep sleep has been quietly sliding though — you opened the stretch with 49 and 51 minutes of deep, and by the last few nights it's drifted down to the high twenties and low thirties, so that's the one thing worth keeping an eye on as you dial the routine tighter. Overall the trajectory is pointing the right direction, and whatever you were doing going into that July 3 night, do that again.
